title: Integrate trait runtime hooks into bullet tick loop (onTick/onHit/onBlockHit/onExpire/onRemove)

reference_files.reason: Trait runtime hooks - 5 hook types, trigger timing, onExpire vs onRemove relationship (onExpire then onRemove for EXPIRED), hook registration and execution
Step 10: Hook trigger points - onTick per tick, onHit after damage applied, onBlockHit on block hit, onExpire on range exceeded, onRemove on any removal
onHit timing - damage already applied, onHit for附加效果 only, chain behavior for penetration (onHit(A) modifications carry to B)

acceptance_criteria: BulletTickHandler (from subtask 09) updated to call trait hooks at appropriate points via TraitHookRegistry
onTick: called at start of each bullet's tick processing (before position advancement), callbacks receive (BulletRecord, BulletSnapshot)
onHit: called AFTER damage application (subtask 12) when entity hit occurs, callbacks receive (BulletRecord, BulletSnapshot, Entity target)
onBlockHit: called when block hit occurs (before penetration handling), callbacks receive (BulletRecord, BulletSnapshot, BlockPos, Direction)
onExpire: called when bullet exceeds range or enters unloaded chunk, callbacks receive (BulletRecord, BulletSnapshot)
onRemove: called before bullet is removed from BulletManager for ANY reason, callbacks receive (BulletRecord, BulletSnapshot, RemoveReason)
When bullet expires (EXPIRED reason): onExpire triggers first, then onRemove with RemoveReason.EXPIRED
Other removal reasons (HIT_ENTITY, HIT_BLOCK, UNLOADED_CHUNK, MANUAL, DIMENSION_UNLOAD): only onRemove triggers
Hook execution: TraitHookRegistry.getHooks(traitId, hookType) returns list of callbacks, all executed in registration order
All registered hooks for all trait IDs are checked and executed (hooks don't depend on trait being true - callback internally checks snapshot.getTrait)
